title: Perfect state transfer in Grover walks on dihedral Cayley graphs
authors: Koushik Bhakta, Bikash Bhattacharjya, Xiwang Cao
abstract: The paper investigates perfect state transfer (PST) in Grover walks on Cayley graphs over the dihedral group $D_n$. The Grover walk is a discrete-time quantum walk widely studied in quantum information processing. A Cayley graph $\operatorname{Cay}(Γ,S)$ is called normal if $S$ is the union of some conjugacy classes of the group $Γ$; otherwise, it is called non-normal. Most existing studies have been restricted to Cayley graphs over abelian groups. In contrast, we investigate both normal and non-normal cases for Cayley graphs over the non-abelian group $D_n$. By examining the parity of $n$ and the normality of the Cayley graph, we obtain a complete characterization of PST on $\operatorname{Cay}(D_n,S)$. In particular, we establish necessary and sufficient conditions for the occurrence of PST in all possible cases, and prove that PST does not occur for normal Cayley graphs when $n$ is odd. Furthermore, we construct several infinite families of normal and non-normal Cayley graphs $\operatorname{Cay}(D_n,S)$ that exhibit PST, illustrating the application of the main result. Our approach is based on the representation theory of the dihedral group.
summary: This paper provides a complete mathematical classification of when perfect state transfer can occur in Grover walks on dihedral Cayley graphs. By moving beyond abelian groups to the non-abelian dihedral group, it identifies specific graph structures that allow for high-fidelity state transport, which is a fundamental requirement for quantum information processing.
main_problem: The paper seeks to provide a complete characterization of the conditions required for Perfect State Transfer (PST) in Grover walks on Cayley graphs over the non-abelian dihedral group D_n.
main_result: The authors establish necessary and sufficient conditions for PST in both normal and non-normal Cayley graphs, proving that PST cannot occur in normal Cayley graphs when n is odd and constructing infinite families of graphs that do exhibit PST.
method: The study employs the representation theory of the dihedral group, spectral analysis of the transition matrix, and Fourier analysis on finite groups, utilizing Chebyshev polynomials to evaluate the dynamics.
model_or_system: The model is a discrete-time Grover walk on Cayley graphs of the dihedral group D_n, where the evolution is governed by a transition matrix derived from the graph's adjacency structure.
key_observables: Perfect State Transfer (PST) between specific vertices and the periodicity of the quantum walk.
important_parameters: The parity of n, the normality of the connection set S (whether it is a union of conjugacy classes), and the degree of the graph.
main_assumptions: The graphs are assumed to be finite, simple, and symmetric (S = S^-1), and the study is specifically focused on the Grover walk model.
figures_summary: Figure 1 provides a classification flowchart or matrix summarizing the existence of PST based on the normality of the graph and the parity of n.
paper_structure: The paper begins with an introduction to Grover walks and Cayley graphs, followed by the application of representation theory to compute eigenvalues and eigenvectors, then proceeds to a case-by-case analysis of PST conditions for even and odd n, and concludes with specific examples and open questions.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
